targets.recommended_evidence_quality_note: Test-design false-negative on C2 zone_map_coverage. Existing autopsy artifact failure_autopsy_EXQ-577_2026-05-16.md. V3-EXQ-577a PASS supersedes. Run_id added to review_tracker 2026-05-17.
learning_extracted: C2 criterion used per-episode strict invariant against stochastic Voronoi redraw design; 2-3/100 episodes expected to have one base zone absent by design
V3-EXQ-577a redefined C2 as aggregate coverage + redraw guard; passed all seeds

targets.recommended_evidence_quality_note: Runtime anomaly: SD-055 CEM change (commit 5fdba4c) landed between the 19:59Z PASS run (cloud-2) and 20:50Z FAIL run (cloud-1), causing early termination at 70 steps for ARM_1/2/3 seed=7 and ARM_3 seed=42. ARM_1 canonical (no forced injection) also terminated at 70 steps for seed=7, ruling out MECH-216 seam failure. MECH-216 schema_salience seam confirmed functional by concurrent PASS run (20260515T195934Z, all arms 1200 steps, P1/P2/P3 PASS, ARM_3 wanting=1008.56).
learning_extracted: MECH-216 schema_salience -> update_schema_wanting -> residue_field seam functional (confirmed by PASS run)
Multi-machine claiming can produce duplicate runs with different code versions when substrate changes land between claim and execution
ARM_1 canonical crash ruling out injection as cause is a reliable diagnostic signature for code-version race artifacts
targets.routing: mark-reviewed (both runs); no re-queue needed; seam confirmed

targets.recommended_evidence_quality_note: PASS run confirming MECH-216 seam functional. Marked reviewed together with concurrent FAIL run (runtime anomaly). No claim weighting (claim_ids=[]).

biological_reference.closest_mechanism: Incentive salience (Berridge/Robinson wanting system): tonic DA sustains anticipatory wanting post-consummatory; wanting builds during post-consummatory windows
dependencies: repeated reward contact to build DA trace
biological_reference.divergence: none -- biology supports sustained wanting trace. Failure is implementation-level (cold-start), not theoretical.

targets.recommended_evidence_quality_note: V3-EXQ-582 FAIL (diagnostic, claim_ids=[]): n_contacts_post_warmup=0 across all 12 runs (4 alphas x 3 seeds). EMA trace unmeasurable in post-warmup window. OFF anchor (alpha=1.0) reproduces EXQ-536a collapse (0.005) -- instrumentation correct, regime valid. Failure is contact-density / warm-start dependent: z_goal_active_fraction=0.0 across all arms; agent makes 2-4 chance contacts in 4000 eval steps, all pre-warmup. Option 1 EMA not refuted -- warm-start z_goal bootstrap is the missing prerequisite. Per interpretation grid: escalate to Option 2 + warm-start arm (transient_benefit_enabled or forced z_goal seeding). pending_retest_after_substrate=true (infant_substrate transient-benefit + z_goal seeding combination required).

recommended_substrate_queue_entry.note: Option 2 + warm-start arm experiment required. Must include: (1) transient_benefit_enabled=True arm for contact density, (2) Option 2 drive_floor / insatiability arm, (3) Option 1 EMA arm for comparison, (4) OFF arm reproducing 536a. Acceptance must gate on n_contacts_post_warmup > 0 before interpreting EMA trace.

learning_extracted: Option 1 (EMA) not refuted: untestable without benefit contacts. Warm-start z_goal bootstrap is the missing prerequisite for both Option 1 and Option 2.
OFF anchor discipline confirmed: alpha=1.0 reproduces EXQ-536a (0.005). Measurement infrastructure trustworthy.

Chicken-and-egg cold-start: z_goal seeding requires benefit contact + drive amplification; agent cannot contact benefits without z_goal-driven seeking. Transient benefit patches (infant_substrate GAP-3) are the designed solution.
Script interpretation grid row 'No arm clears A1 -> Option 2' is correct routing but assumes contact-dense regime; warm-start arm required to satisfy that assumption.
All 12 post-warmup contact counts = 0: this is a structural property of the regime, not a per-seed or per-alpha anomaly.

cluster_pattern.shape: goal-pipeline infra: two diagnostic FAILs in the goal-seeding / MECH-216 seam stack. Not a shared scientific failure -- EXQ-566 FAIL is a runtime anomaly; EXQ-582 FAIL is a contact-density / warm-start prereq gap. EXQ-577 FAIL is an infrastructure test-criterion specification gap. No convergent claim-level failure pattern.
